description: The numeral representing completion of fundamental cycles, the stability of cardinal directions, the rhythm of seasons. More than simple quantity, 'kore' acknowledges the profound organizing principle of four—the way reality arranges itself in quarters, creating both stability and movement through return.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'k', the light velar stop that creates clear definition, like marking cardinal points. This meets 'o', the round vowel of wholeness and cycles. The transition through 'r', the rolling liquid, to 'e', the clear focused vowel, creates movement within stability. The progression from defined wholeness ('ko') to rolling clarity ('re') mirrors how four works—stable structure that enables cyclic movement.
image_prompt: Art Nouveau watercolor: Four equal quadrants emerge from center point, each washed in seasonal palette - spring greens, summer golds, autumn rusts, winter blues. Mucha's decorative circles at cardinal points connected by flowing curved lines, not rigid crosses. Transparent overlapping creates new colors where seasons meet. Botanical motifs in each quarter - budding branches, full blooms, falling leaves, bare elegant stems. Compass rose suggested through organic forms. Soft wet-on-wet transitions between quadrants, no hard boundaries. Pearl and ivory highlights mark the crossing center. Style: Mandala meets Art Nouveau seasonal calendar in delicate watercolor.
grammatical_notes: Functions as the fourth cardinal numeral, marking the first number associated with completeness and return. Requires the plural particle 'lo' when modifying nouns. Often appears in expressions related to wholeness, cycles, and natural rhythms. May have special uses in seasonal references, directional systems, and time divisions.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures organize by fours—seasons turning, cardinal directions, phases of the moon, times of day. 'Kore' carries this deep recognition that four creates the stable framework for cyclic existence.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Four embodies fundamental teachings—the Four Noble Truths that diagnose and cure suffering. Like these truths that create a complete system, 'kore' represents wholeness through systematic completeness.

tags.temporal: organizes time through seasons, day quarters, and lunar phases
tags.nature: appears in natural patterns from directions to seasonal cycles
tags.spatial: defines cardinal directions creating framework for orientation
tags.cognition: represents systematic completeness and quaternary thinking
